name: adduser
title: adduser
summary: add and remove users and groups
description:
add and remove users and groups
This package includes the 'adduser' and 'deluser' commands for creating
and removing users.
.
- 'adduser' creates new users and groups and adds existing users to
existing groups;
- 'deluser' removes users and groups and removes users from a given
group.
.
Adding users with 'adduser' is much easier than adding them manually.
'Adduser' will choose UID and GID values that conform to Debian policy,
create a home directory, copy skeletal user configuration, and
automate setting initial values for the user's password, real name
and so on.
.
'Deluser' can back up and remove users' home directories
and mail spool or all the files they own on the system.
.
A custom script can be executed after each of the commands.
.
'Adduser' and 'Deluser' are intended to be used by the local
administrator in lieu of the tools from the 'useradd' suite, and
they provide support for easy use from Debian package maintainer
scripts, functioning as kind of a policy layer to make those scripts
easier and more stable to write and maintain.

commands.name: deluser
commands.description: remove a user or group from the system

commands.name: addgroup
commands.description: add or manipulate users or groups

commands.name: delgroup
commands.description: remove a user or group from the system

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use adduser because add and remove users and groups.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use adduser as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `adduser [--add-extra-groups] [--allow-all-names] [--allow-bad-names] [--comment comment]` as documented upstream for adduser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `adduser --system [--comment comment] [--conf file] [--debug] [--gid id] [--group] [--home dir]` as documented upstream for adduser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `adduser --unlock [--system] user` as documented upstream for adduser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `adduser --group [--conf file] [--debug] [--firstgid id] [--gid ID] [--lastgid id] [--quiet]` as documented upstream for adduser.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair adduser with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `adduser --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: adduser, addgroup - add or manipulate users or groups
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install adduser. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `adduser --system [--comment comment] [--conf file] [--debug] [--gid id] [--group] [--home dir]`. Further invocations from upstream: `adduser --unlock [--system] user`; `adduser --group [--conf file] [--debug] [--firstgid id] [--gid ID] [--lastgid id] [--quiet]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: add and remove users and groups.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: adduser, addgroup - add or manipulate users or groups.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install adduser
Install with: sudo apt install adduser
